title: dispatching-parallel-agents

goal.text: Split genuinely independent work across focused agents while preserving safe scoping and integration review.

requirements.summary: Use parallel delegation only when multiple tasks are independent enough to benefit from concurrent agents.
acceptanceCriteria: The workflow distinguishes related vs independent domains before dispatching.
The workflow distinguishes parallel-safe vs sequential-only delegation.
Each agent prompt is focused, self-contained, and explicit about outputs.
Returned work is reviewed and integrated before completion is claimed.

tasks.title: Identify independent domains and choose the dispatch mode
tasks.detail:
Assess the current failures or tasks, group them by domain, and decide whether they should be handled together, sequentially, or in parallel.

Use [CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md](/D:/Users/chany/Documents/claw-kit/packages/codex-adapter/skills/dispatching-parallel-agents/CLAW-KNOWLEDGE.md) for the canonical branching rules.

parallel.summary: The domains are independent and safe for parallel dispatch.
nextsteps: Create one focused agent task per domain.

sequential.summary: The domains are distinct but should not run concurrently.
nextsteps: Still split by domain, but do not dispatch them at the same time.
Preserve the same prompt quality requirements.

investigate-together.summary: The work likely shares a root cause and should be investigated together.
nextsteps: Do not fan out into parallel agents yet.
Keep the work in one coordinated investigation until the root cause boundary is clearer.

tasks.title: Design focused agent prompts and dispatch safely
tasks.detail:
For each chosen domain, write a narrow, self-contained prompt with context, constraints, and expected output. Then dispatch according to the chosen mode.

Do not use vague or broad prompts such as "fix everything".

default.summary: Dispatch phase completed; proceed to review and integration.
nextsteps: Check whether any returned changes overlap or conflict before integrating them.

tasks.title: Review, integrate, and verify the returned work
tasks.detail:
Review what each agent returned, integrate the non-conflicting work, and run the appropriate verification after integration.

This step must preserve the source skill's emphasis on conflict checks and full-suite or relevant verification after dispatch.

references.why: Preserves the decision tree, prompt-shape requirements, and anti-patterns for the converted skill.
Keeps the full original superpowers explanations and worked example beside the claw entry.
The copied agent descriptor remains part of the converted skill package.

rules: Do not dispatch in parallel until independence is established.
Do not split related failures across multiple agents just because there are several failing tests.
Each agent prompt must be focused, self-contained, and explicit about expected output.
Review and integrate returned work before claiming success.
Use SUPERPOWERS-FALLBACK.md whenever exact source wording or the worked example matters.
